description:
A small dataset stored in /home/user/data needs to be profiled before wider
  processing.  Two CSV files are already present:

    • /home/user/data/sales_q1.csv
    • /home/user/data/sales_q2.csv

  Your goal is to benchmark how long your preferred row-counting method
  ( wc, awk, Python, csvkit … any is fine ) takes on each file and to record the
  results in a single, machine-readable log.

  1. Measure the **elapsed wall-clock time** required to count the number of
     rows in each CSV file (include the header row in the count).
  2. Create the directory /home/user/benchmarks if it does not exist.
  3. Produce the log file
        /home/user/benchmarks/row_count_benchmark.log
     in the exact CSV format shown below.  This log will be parsed by an
     automated checker, so the layout **must** match precisely:

        FILE,ROWS,ELAPSED_MS
        sales_q1.csv,11,<elapsed_time_in_ms>
        sales_q2.csv,8,<elapsed_time_in_ms>

     • There is **one comma** between columns and **no extra spaces**.
     • The first line is the header exactly as written.
     • The second and third lines must appear in that order: sales_q1.csv
       first, then sales_q2.csv.
     • <elapsed_time_in_ms> must be an **integer** giving the total elapsed
       time, in milliseconds, rounded up to the nearest millisecond.
       (Any integer ≥0 is acceptable; the checker only verifies that it is a
       valid non-negative integer.)

  Hints (not mandatory):
     • `/usr/bin/time -f "%e"` or `date +%s%3N` can be helpful for timing.
     • Make sure you count the number of lines accurately; the expected values
       are 11 and 8 respectively.

  When you are done, the checker will look only at the file
  /home/user/benchmarks/row_count_benchmark.log.  No additional output is
  required.
truth:
1. Pre-existing files and their exact line counts:

     /home/user/data/sales_q1.csv      → 11 lines
     /home/user/data/sales_q2.csv      →  8 lines

  2. Exact contents supplied in the task image:

     --- /home/user/data/sales_q1.csv ---
     id,region,sales,week
     1,North,200,1
     2,South,180,1
     3,East,220,1
     4,West,210,1
     5,North,205,2
     6,South,190,2
     7,East,230,2
     8,West,215,2
     9,North,210,3
     10,South,195,3
     ------------------------------------

     --- /home/user/data/sales_q2.csv ---
     id,region,sales,week
     11,East,225,4
     12,West,220,4
     13,North,215,5
     14,South,200,5
     15,East,235,6
     16,West,225,6
     17,North,220,7
     ------------------------------------

     (Each line, including the final one, ends with a single newline character
      “\n”.)

  3. Verification rules applied by the automated test:

     • /home/user/benchmarks/row_count_benchmark.log must exist.
     • The file must contain exactly three lines:
         line 1: “FILE,ROWS,ELAPSED_MS”
         line 2: /^sales_q1\.csv,11,[0-9]+$/
         line 3: /^sales_q2\.csv,8,[0-9]+$/
     • No extra spaces, tabs, or blank lines are permitted.
     • The ELAPSED_MS column may be any non-negative integer; only its
       syntactic validity is checked.

  4. No other files or directories are evaluated.
